body:
Hey, I forwarded you an invoice from Coastal Cellars that came through our shared inbox this morning. It's for $3,840 — does that match what you were expecting for the last shipment?

Let me know if it looks off and I can follow up with them directly.
snippet: Hey, I forwarded you an invoice from Coastal Cellars that came through our share...
subject: Supplier invoice

body:
Got it, thanks. That's actually $240 more than the agreed rate — I think they may have added a fuel surcharge without notifying us first.

I'll reach out to their accounts team today and sort it out. I'll let you know what they say.

— Catherine
snippet: Got it, thanks. That's actually $240 more than the agreed rate — I think they ma...
subject: Re: Supplier invoice

body:
Hey, Sophie texted me saying she can't make her Saturday afternoon shift — something about a family thing. Do you have a backup lined up, or do you need me to come in?

Just let me know and I can move some things around.
snippet: Hey, Sophie texted me saying she can't make her Saturday afternoon shift — somet...
subject: Staffing schedule

body:
Ugh, two days' notice. I'll check with Marcus first — he mentioned wanting extra hours last week.

If Marcus can't do it, would you really be okay to come in? It would be the 1–6 shift. I hate to ask on a weekend.
snippet: Ugh, two days' notice. I'll check with Marcus first — he mentioned wanting extra...
subject: Re: Staffing schedule

body:
Good news — Marcus texted back and he can cover it, no problem. So you're all set!

Should I mention to Sophie that she needs to give more notice next time, or would you rather that come from you?
snippet: Good news — Marcus texted back and he can cover it, no problem. So you're all se...
subject: Re: Staffing schedule

body:
Hey, just a heads up — the Q2 estimated tax payment is due June 15. I know things have been hectic at the shop, but wanted to make sure you had it on your radar.

Have you talked to Karen about the estimated amount? I can reach out to her if that's easier.
snippet: Hey, just a heads up — the Q2 estimated tax payment is due June 15. I know thing...
subject: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
Thanks for the reminder, I completely lost track of the date. I'll email Karen today and ask her to run the numbers.

Can you check the business account balance? Last time it was around $4,200 so I want to make sure we're not cutting it close.
snippet: Thanks for the reminder, I completely lost track of the date. I'll email Karen t...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
Just heard back from Karen — she's estimating $4,650 for Q2 based on Q1 revenue. Account balance is fine, plenty of runway.

She'll send the payment voucher by end of week. I'll save a copy for our records.
snippet: Just heard back from Karen — she's estimating $4,650 for Q2 based on Q1 revenue....
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
Perfect, thanks for handling that. Once the voucher comes through, go ahead and submit the payment.

Also remind me to set up quarterly calendar reminders so we stop scrambling like this every time.

— Catherine
snippet: Perfect, thanks for handling that. Once the voucher comes through, go ahead and ...
subject: Re: Quarterly tax estimate

body:
Did you see the Google review that came in yesterday? Someone named 'WineEnthusiast42' left two stars and said the staff was 'unhelpful' and the selection was 'disappointing.'

I think you should respond publicly — it's the kind of thing that sticks if you leave it. Want me to draft something?
snippet: Did you see the Google review that came in yesterday? Someone named 'WineEnthusi...
subject: Customer review reply

body:
I did see it and it stung. I honestly don't know who the customer was — the description doesn't match any interaction I remember from the weekend.

Yes, please draft a response. Keep it professional and empathetic but don't be overly apologetic if we didn't actually do anything wrong. I'll review before we post.
snippet: I did see it and it stung. I honestly don't know who the customer was — the desc...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
Here's a draft:

"Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We're genuinely sorry to hear your visit didn't meet your expectations. Our team works hard to create a welcoming experience and we'd love the chance to make it right. Please reach out to us at hello@atkinswines.com so we can connect with you directly."

Short and non-defensive. Let me know what you think.
snippet:
Here's a draft:

"Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We're ge...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
That's really good. One small tweak — can the last sentence read 'so we can connect with you directly' instead of referencing the visit? It sounds less like we're interrogating them.

Otherwise I love it. Warm but not groveling.
snippet: That's really good. One small tweak — can the last sentence read 'so we can conn...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
Updated and posted — the response is live now. I also flagged the review as potentially inaccurate, just in case Google will look at it.

Hopefully it defuses things. You handled this the right way.
snippet: Updated and posted — the response is live now. I also flagged the review as pote...
subject: Re: Customer review reply

body:
Hey, are you free for lunch any day this week? I feel like we haven't had a proper sit-down meal together in forever. My schedule is pretty flexible Wednesday through Friday.

I was thinking we could finally try that Thai place on Elm Street.
snippet: Hey, are you free for lunch any day this week? I feel like we haven't had a prop...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Thursday works perfectly — I have a slow morning and don't need to be at the shop until 3. Thai sounds great, I've been wanting to try it.

Noon-ish? I can meet you there. — Catherine
snippet: Thursday works perfectly — I have a slow morning and don't need to be at the sho...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hey, I have a slow morning next Tuesday and thought it'd be nice to grab coffee before the week gets away from us. You don't open until 11 on Tuesdays, right?

Let me know if you're up for it.
snippet: Hey, I have a slow morning next Tuesday and thought it'd be nice to grab coffee ...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Yes, that sounds really nice. I've been meaning to tell you about the new supplier I'm looking at and would love your input.

Where were you thinking? Somewhere close to home or somewhere new?
snippet: Yes, that sounds really nice. I've been meaning to tell you about the new suppli...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
How about Roast & Co. on Fifth? I know you like their oat milk lattes and they have those almond croissants you love. 9am work?

Also looking forward to hearing about the supplier — sounds like something interesting is brewing (no pun intended).
snippet: How about Roast & Co. on Fifth? I know you like their oat milk lattes and they h...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Hey, your mom's birthday is July 12th and I think we should do something really nice this year. She mentioned wanting to try Magnolia House — their weekend brunch looks amazing and they do a prix fixe.

Should I look into making a reservation? We'll probably need to book soon for a good table.
snippet: Hey, your mom's birthday is July 12th and I think we should do something really ...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body:
She would absolutely love Magnolia House. Yes, please book — I'm thinking the 12th at 11am so we're done before afternoon traffic.

I'll handle the cake. Mira's Bakery does that lemon olive oil cake she loves. Can you check if they need advance notice for a custom order?
snippet: She would absolutely love Magnolia House. Yes, please book — I'm thinking the 12...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Done! Reservation set for July 12th at 11am, table for 6. I emailed Mira's too — they need 5 days' notice for a custom cake, so we're well within the window.

Should we tell your mom or keep it a surprise until the day of?
snippet: Done! Reservation set for July 12th at 11am, table for 6. I emailed Mira's too —...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Let's tell her — she stresses out if she doesn't know what's coming. I'll call her tonight.

This is going to be really lovely. Thanks for thinking of it, you always do. — Catherine
snippet: Let's tell her — she stresses out if she doesn't know what's coming. I'll call h...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Hey, the renewal notice for the shop's POS system came in today. They're offering three tiers this year — Basic ($89/mo), Professional ($149/mo), and Enterprise ($219/mo). We're currently on Basic.

I'll forward the full breakdown, but wanted to flag it early since the contract expires July 31.
snippet: Hey, the renewal notice for the shop's POS system came in today. They're offerin...
subject: Renewal options

body:
Thanks for flagging. What does Professional add over Basic? Last time I looked, the main differences were inventory analytics and multi-location support, neither of which I really need.

Is there a discount if we pre-pay annually?
snippet: Thanks for flagging. What does Professional add over Basic? Last time I looked, ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Professional adds an analytics dashboard, employee time tracking, and a loyalty program module. Multi-location is Enterprise only.

Annual pre-pay is 15% off, so Professional comes to about $129/mo billed annually vs $149 monthly. Honestly the loyalty program module alone might be worth it given how often you mention wanting one for regulars.
snippet: Professional adds an analytics dashboard, employee time tracking, and a loyalty ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
The loyalty module is compelling — I've had several regulars ask about a rewards program this year. Let's go with Professional, annual billing.

Can you handle the renewal? And make sure it goes to the business card, not the personal one.
snippet: The loyalty module is compelling — I've had several regulars ask about a rewards...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
All done — Professional plan, annual billing, business card on file. Confirmation number is RN-48821.

Also signed us up for the loyalty program onboarding session: 30-minute walkthrough next Thursday at 2pm. I'll forward you the calendar invite.
snippet: All done — Professional plan, annual billing, business card on file. Confirmatio...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Hey, got an invoice from Vineyard Direct for $1,120. That's higher than usual — did you order anything extra recently, or is this leftover from the tasting event?

Just want to confirm it's accurate before I approve it.
snippet: Hey, got an invoice from Vineyard Direct for $1,120. That's higher than usual — ...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body:
Yes, that's correct — I ordered two extra cases of the Sonoma Pinot for the tasting event and had them rush ship it. The rush fee added about $80, which explains the difference.

Go ahead and approve it. I'll note it in the event budget so it's accounted for. — Catherine
snippet: Yes, that's correct — I ordered two extra cases of the Sonoma Pinot for the tast...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body:
Hey, the allocation offer from Silverton Estate came in today. They're offering us 18 cases of the 2022 reserve Cabernet at last year's pricing, but the acceptance window closes July 15.

Thought you'd want to move on this quickly — it sells through fast every year and 18 is more than they gave us last time.
snippet: Hey, the allocation offer from Silverton Estate came in today. They're offering ...
subject: Silverton allocation offer

body:
18 cases is generous — last year they only allocated us 12.

Are they requiring we take the full allocation, or can we split it? And did they confirm delivery timing? I want it in the warehouse well before the fall tasting series kicks off.
snippet:
18 cases is generous — last year they only allocated us 12.

Are they requiring ...
subject: Re: Silverton allocation offer

body:
Full allocation or nothing this year — they're tightening up across all their accounts. Delivery would be early September, which lands perfectly ahead of the fall lineup.

I'm inclined to take all 18; we'll move it without a problem. Let's confirm this weekend and I'll send Silverton our acceptance before the window closes.
snippet: Full allocation or nothing this year — they're tightening up across all their ac...
subject: Re: Silverton allocation offer

body:
Passing along a few things from my reading this week that I thought you'd find useful.

Wine Business Monthly had a solid piece on how small retailers are using Instagram Reels for weekly selections — thought of you immediately. I'll forward the link.
snippet: Passing along a few things from my reading this week that I thought you'd find u...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Oh please send that link! I've been thinking about doing something with Reels but wasn't sure what format actually works for a wine shop.

What else was in your reading pile?
snippet: Oh please send that link! I've been thinking about doing something with Reels bu...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Link sent! Also read an HBR piece on customer retention in specialty retail — lots of data on loyalty programs, which feels timely given the POS renewal we just handled.

And there's a fun Food & Wine piece on natural wine trends for summer that might give you some buying ideas. Want me to forward that one too?
snippet: Link sent! Also read an HBR piece on customer retention in specialty retail — lo...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Yes, send all of it! I'm saving these for my train ride Thursday.

You always find the most useful stuff. Maybe you should do a real weekly digest — I'd actually subscribe. — Catherine
snippet:
Yes, send all of it! I'm saving these for my train ride Thursday.

You always fi...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Hey, can we sort out the pickup schedule for this week? I have a client meeting Monday afternoon I can't move, so I won't be able to get them from school that day.

Can you cover Monday, or should I ask your mom?
snippet: Hey, can we sort out the pickup schedule for this week? I have a client meeting ...
subject: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
I can do Monday — I'll move my supplier call to the morning. But I have a wine delivery coming in Tuesday afternoon, so I need you to cover that one.

What does your Wednesday look like?
snippet: I can do Monday — I'll move my supplier call to the morning. But I have a wine d...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Tuesday is fine, I've got it. Wednesday I'm in the city for a design review and probably won't be back until 5:30 at the earliest — that's cutting it close.

Should I ask your mom if she can grab them Wednesday? I think she's usually free in the afternoons.
snippet: Tuesday is fine, I've got it. Wednesday I'm in the city for a design review and ...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Yes, ask Mom — she loves doing pickups and I'm pretty sure she's free Wednesdays. Actually, I'll just text her now, it's faster.

Thursday and Friday should be fine between the two of us, those days are more flexible.
snippet: Yes, ask Mom — she loves doing pickups and I'm pretty sure she's free Wednesdays...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Your mom confirmed Wednesday — and she's taking them for ice cream after, so they'll be thrilled.

Full week sorted: you Monday, me Tuesday, your mom Wednesday, you Thursday, me Friday. Adding it to the shared calendar now. Shout if anything changes.
snippet: Your mom confirmed Wednesday — and she's taking them for ice cream after, so the...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Hey, I was looking at the calendar and thinking the last two weeks of August might work for the family trip we keep putting off. Kids are still off school and things tend to slow down for me work-wise that time of year.

Is there any chance we could make 7–10 days work? I was thinking somewhere driveable — maybe the coast?
snippet: Hey, I was looking at the calendar and thinking the last two weeks of August mig...
subject: Family vacation planning

body:
August 16–24 actually looks manageable — Marcus and Sophie could handle the shop if I prep them well, and I can check in remotely on busy days.

Mendocino? The kids love the beach there and it's only 3.5 hours. We could book the inn we stayed at two summers ago — let's lock in the dates and I'll sort out the lodging. — Catherine
snippet: August 16–24 actually looks manageable — Marcus and Sophie could handle the shop...
subject: Re: Family vacation planning

body:
Hey, do we have anything locked in for the weekend? I don't think we do, but wanted to check before I committed to anything.

I'm open to whatever — could be low-key or we could do something with the kids if you have the energy for it.
snippet: Hey, do we have anything locked in for the weekend? I don't think we do, but wan...
subject: Weekend plans

body:
Nothing on the calendar as far as I know. I was thinking Saturday morning we hit the farmers market — the kids love it and we're almost out of that good sourdough from the bread vendor.

Then maybe a movie Saturday evening? Something easy. Sunday can stay totally open.
snippet: Nothing on the calendar as far as I know. I was thinking Saturday morning we hit...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
Farmers market and a movie sounds like a perfect Saturday. The kids have been asking to see that new animated one, so maybe that's the pick.

I'll handle dinner — I'll grab whatever looks good at the market. Let's leave Sunday wide open and see how we feel.
snippet: Farmers market and a movie sounds like a perfect Saturday. The kids have been as...

body: Catherine, heads up on the Italian inventory. Our portfolio tracker still lists the Tenuta Marchetti Barolo 2014 allocation at 20 cases on hand, which is the figure we carried before the summer shipment. I logged the receiving myself when the pallet arrived from Milano last month, so the tracker is running behind. Please treat that tracker number as stale until IT syncs it against the warehouse dock count, which is what we actually counted in.
snippet: Catherine, heads up on the Italian inventory. Our portfolio tracker still lists...
subject: Barolo inventory count looks off

body: Thanks Eric. I will trust the receiving count from the dock over the tracker figure and get the Italian inventory reconciled before I finalize the fall allocation. Please send me the delivery confirmation for the Barolo pallet so I have the exact case count on record.
snippet: Thanks Eric. I will trust the receiving count from the dock over the tracker fig...
subject: Re: Barolo inventory count looks off

body: Catherine, following up on our conversation, Le Jardin Dore wants to expand our Burgundy-style program significantly for the coming year. We are now projecting demand for roughly 400 cases across the Burgundy-style reds, well above the smaller allocation we ran last season. I would like Atkins & Associates to be our primary source if you can commit to that volume. Can we review availability against your fall allocation and confirm what you can hold for us?
snippet: Catherine, following up on our conversation, Le Jardin Dore wants to expand our...
subject: Le Jardin Dore expanded Burgundy program

body: David, thank you for the update. A 400-case Burgundy-style program is a meaningful step up from Le Jardin Dore's prior projected demand, and I want to make sure we can support it properly. Let me reconcile it against my fall allocation and current receivables and I will come back to you this week with what we can commit.

subject: Re: Le Jardin Dore expanded Burgundy program
